title: 6H: exercise quality and testing-readiness contract
goal: Define the binding production and independent-review contract for all 111 exercises: concept-led interaction, three distinct lenses, French age-appropriate language, specific feedback, accessibility, responsive behavior, evidence honesty, isolation, and the testing lifecycle ceiling.
acceptance: The contract defines minimum complete states: introduction, active concept manipulation, decision/submission, specific explanatory feedback, recoverable retry or scaffold, continuation, and completion.

The three lenses have distinct purposes and may not be generic reskins: construction makes a relationship visible, practice varies it deliberately, and transfer/diagnosis uses a novel context and records an interpretable error family in local state only.

The accessibility rubric covers full keyboard operation, pointer/touch targets, non-color cues, focus, reduced motion, readable French, 320px responsive behavior, and alternatives to fine-motor drag interactions.

The evidence rubric requires objective/source/statement traceability, observable learning evidence, misconceptions distinguished from hypotheses, bounded content models, observation plans, and no unsupported 6H or validation claim.
The isolation rubric prohibits classroom imports, cross-exercise imports, persistence, analytics, identity, network calls, and substantial copyrighted source content.

The lifecycle rule requires implementation slices to leave work prototyping and permits paired independent review slices to advance passing work only to testing; validated is reserved for pupil trials.

The visual guidance explicitly allows independent studies inspired by high-quality interactive learning while prohibiting copied Brilliant.org content, branding, assets, or proprietary interaction text.
must_ask_if: The pinned decision, research, production-plan, or slice-graph inputs in experiments/design/6h-batch-inputs.json do not match the working baseline.
The quality bar would require a new runtime dependency, shared exercise engine, persistence, network access, or a change to the existing lab isolation contract.
Any requirement conflicts with the approved decision record or would permit automated validated status.
You need to change files outside the declared areas.
